10. Background Story + Story of Discovery: 



---> the more you can connect the story with the UM-P + UM-S, the better









My daughter yelled out for everyone to hear,  “Mommy, will I be fat like you?”

My 13 year old daughter was crying uncontrollably …

She screamed this out in front of about 20 moms and other kids at a birthday party:

“Mommy, will I be fat like you?”

All the other moms' heads turned to look at me …

… their mouths dropped open in complete shock ...

My daughter actually came up to me and punched my belly in anger.

Everyone saw this.

I felt like I just got run over by a truck.

I got nauseous and felt like throwing up right then and there.

My head got dizzy and lightheaded - I felt like I was going to pass out.

This embarrassed the life out of me and broke my heart.

I felt ashamed.

After finally getting my composure, I said “Don’t be silly Julie.  NOw tell me what happened to make you say that?”

Between tears, Julie told me one of hte other kids at the bowling alley party had teased her about being fat “just liek your Mom”.

Oh my God!

I know kids can be mean and frank, especially teenage girls, but xxx xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx

It’s shocking, but not surprising, that children so young are using the word as a weapon to hurt and shame other children. After all, they’ve been watching grown-ups do it all their lives. Children pick up from TV, movies, and books that fat is a synonym for bad.

As a parent it’s heartbreaking to see your child being teased for being fat, and it’s very difficult to know what to say and do.

Body shame and weight-based bullying causes significant distress and cost to young people. It also serves as a risk factor for developing eating disorders.  

All the moms said how terrible it was that someone said this to my little Julie.

I put on a brave face on the outside, but on the inside I was so embarrassed (and heartbroken for my daughter) ..

	I just wanted to get under the covers, curl up into a ball and                       hope this was all just a bad nightmare that wasn’t real! 

I wanted to disappear …

I felt like such a loser …

I had embarrassed and let my only daughter down in front of all of her friends …

I got so depressed.

I couldn’t sleep the night this happened.  I tossed and turned, feeling bad for Julie and for myself.

I got out of bed at 3 AM and looked at my tummy …

I had to be honest with myself when I looked in the mirror …

I looked like I was 7 months pregnant with a double-chin, bloated face, fat arms, thighs full of flab and already a turkey neck.

I hated what I saw when I looked i the mirror.

I had to find the answer to lose weight, not just for myself, but more importantly, I didn’t want my precious daughter Julie to suffer from the fat-shaming, name-calling and bullying that my being overweight would cause her anymore.

I loved my daughter more than life itself and I couldn’t stand to see her suffer anymore if it was MY FAULT.

But wanting to lose weight and actually losing weight and keeping it off are two different things.  Everyone wants to lose weight, but few women do and keep it off.  

I used to be fairly slim -- BEFORE I HIT MENOPAUSE.



After I went into perimenopause and then menopause, forget abou it!

I was one of these “ diet failures”.  Sure I’d lost weight before, in fact, many tiems I’ve lost weight.

But I’d always gain the wight back after I stopped deprviing myself on some impossible tokeep diet or on an exericee program I didn’t have the time, energy or motivation for like some professional athlete.

I had trie dit all …

… I had tried and failed with xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx

I did a strict Keto diet for 4 weeks.  I was so excited to try it, thinking it would would finally be the answer to my weight loss.l  It started off good, losing al little weight, but on the third day, I got the infamous “keto flu”.  

Girl, was it awful.  I had Headache, foggy brain, fatigue, irritability, nausea and I couldn’t sleep.  

Then I became miserable not being able to eat carbs.

No carbs meaning no bread, no or anything made me lethargic and cranky.

I was craving carbs constantly like a madwomen and even after four weeks it didn’t go away.

The Keto diet was a disaster for me.

A couple weeks later, I saw an ad for a “Leptin diet” in Womens World magazine.  It said menopause hot flashes and wight gain were caused by the hormone leptin.  It made sense so I bought.

I had high hopes for this, but after 3 months of it, all I had to show for it was more headaches and a weight loss of a whopping 2 pounds!

I”d failed again.

My hot flashes and weight gain were as bad as ever.

I was at my wit’s end ...

***********************************   end of personal story  *************************************





***********************  start of discovery story **************************************************

I prayed and prayed for a solution.

I even asked the minister at my church to pray for me,

I needed a lucky break, and I finally got one.

i was working in an insurance company as an office manager

the company owner had a daughter who went to baylor and was now a medical doctor

he visited his dad in the insurance agency one day

she overheard me talking about what happened to my daughter and how i needing to lose weight

she said he knew this doctor at baylor university who just made a huge new weight loss discovery

I asked her what this weight loss discovery was …

Dr. Rosenthal was very excited about it and told me the details ... 



(#############################  then UM section, ########################)









Inbox







Jan 1, 2021, 2:08 AM (1 day ago)





























































1. Who’s the Audience? 





2. What are the pain points, or biggest fears?

(+ identify the 1-3 HUGE ones, while the rest are secondary)



- Short Term: 





-Long Term:











3. So by reversing the pain points/biggest fears, what is the one big promise (1-3 sentences) you are going to make…how this will change their lives forever?







4. What solutions for the reader’s problem (s) exist already (and why aren’t they working for them)?





5. What’s the new claim/root cause/surprising info? And how does this new solution we’re offering them work? {Mechanism}



---> Add any proof elements here: 

---> Any Mechanism Names here:  





6. Paradoxical Question:

	Rooted in the mechanism, these are questions that get the reader thinking



7. Metaphor:



Joe consistently beats Tom in golf even though “on paper” Tom is a much better golfer.  Tom drives the ball farther, more accurate and even putts better.  How could this be?













8. Bold Claim 

(What’s the huge promise about the results they’re going to get using this product/solution?):

---> Dimensionalize your benefits!







9. What is the Product? 

(If you can write out fascinations here, it will help you immensely. This is the part where you get to shape exactly what the product we’re selling will include)





















11. Some Headline Ideas: 

(One sentence – something that would be the first line of the VSL, or in the copy above the video screen. Attention grabbing)









12. Ideas for a potential upsell product?

(Something that could be sold to the customer immediately after purchasing the main product. You’d want this to be related to product, but solve an additional problem, or provide a significant additional benefit).
